name: Taweret
description: Taweret is a powerful and benevolent Egyptian goddess, often depicted as a composite creature with the head of a hippopotamus, the limbs of a lion, and the tail of a crocodile. Despite her fearsome appearance, she was revered as a protector of women and children, particularly during pregnancy and childbirth. Her protective nature stemmed from her association with the fierce power of these animals, harnessed for benevolent purposes. Taweret was believed to ward off evil spirits and dangers that threatened mothers and newborns. She was also associated with fertility and rebirth, symbolizing the life-giving force of the Nile River. Her imagery often includes symbols of protection and abundance, such as the sa hieroglyph (symbolizing protection) and the ankh (symbolizing life). While her origins are somewhat obscure, Taweret's popularity grew significantly throughout Egyptian history, becoming a prominent figure in household shrines and amulets. She represents the protective and nurturing aspects of the divine feminine, offering comfort and security to those in need. Her enduring presence in Egyptian art and religious practices highlights her importance as a guardian and a symbol of life's continuity.
subtitle: Protector of women and children.
appearance: Taweret is depicted as a standing hippopotamus with a bipedal stance. She possesses the head of a female hippopotamus, complete with large teeth and a prominent snout. Her limbs are those of a lion, and she has the tail of a crocodile. Often, she is shown with pendulous breasts, symbolizing her role as a nurturing mother. She frequently carries the sa hieroglyph, representing protection, and sometimes the ankh, symbolizing life.
habitat: Taweret's influence was felt throughout Egypt, particularly in homes and temples dedicated to childbirth and fertility. She was not associated with a specific physical habitat but rather a spiritual presence invoked during vulnerable times.
behavior: Taweret is portrayed as a fiercely protective and nurturing goddess. She is vigilant in guarding against threats to pregnant women, newborns, and children. While her appearance might be intimidating, her intentions are always benevolent. She is a symbol of strength and reassurance in times of uncertainty.

keyMyths.title: Taweret's Protective Role
keyMyths.description: Taweret was invoked by pregnant women and new mothers to protect them and their children from evil spirits and complications during childbirth. Amulets depicting Taweret were commonly worn for protection.
keyMyths.source: Various Egyptian texts and archaeological findings

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Egyptian deities, including Taweret.
sources.text: Comprehensive overview of Egyptian deities, including Taweret.

sources.author: Emily Teeter
